title: @mysten/pas dependency uses caret range ^0.0.3 — unstable 0.x versioning
description: The `@mysten/pas` client is pinned to `^0.0.3`. npm's caret for 0.x versions allows patch and minor bumps (up to but not including 0.1.0 per npm semver, but effectively any 0.0.x is allowed under some resolvers). An unstable pre-1.0 package should be pinned exactly to avoid silent upgrades that change template derivation or account address computation.
impact: Drifting client version could break autoresolve template wiring (mismatch between on-chain PAS rev pinned in lockfile vs client version). Silent breakage at runtime.
recommendation: Pin exactly: `"@mysten/pas": "0.0.3"` (no caret). Document the compatibility matrix with the on-chain PAS rev in the README.

title: token_template_pas/Move.toml lists empty [dependencies] section
description: `[dependencies]` is declared but empty. The Move toolchain supplies std / sui framework implicitly. An empty section is stylistically noisy; either omit the section or populate explicitly for clarity.
recommendation: Remove the `[dependencies]` header, or add an inline comment: '# std and sui framework supplied implicitly'.

title: scripts/src/deployPasTokenTemplate/index.ts — trivial re-export
description: The file is a trivial one-line re-export. No logic to review. Included only for coverage completeness.
evidence:
import { deployPasTokenTemplate } from './deployPasTokenTemplate'

export default deployPasTokenTemplate;

title: scripts/src/operations/index.ts — re-exports include PAS helpers; no validation
description: Trivial barrel file. Re-exports `registerPasAsset`, `deployAndRegisterPasAsset`, `addToWhitelist`, `removeFromWhitelist`, `mintPasTokens`. No seize/burn/setWhitelistSend/Receive — matches the gaps identified in R5-014 and R5-015.
impact: None per se; re-exports track the operation set.
recommendation: Update as operation helpers are added for seize, burn, setWhitelistSend, setWhitelistReceive (see R5-014, R5-015).
evidence: export { registerPasAsset, deployAndRegisterPasAsset, addToWhitelist, removeFromWhitelist, mintPasTokens } from './pas';

title: getBytecode.ts embeds coin template bytecode as a hex string — provenance not documented
description: The deploy helper ships a hard-coded 1.5 KB hex bytecode constant representing the compiled token_template_pas/sources/token_template.move. No comment or script records how this was generated, nor an invariant that it matches the source in `token_template_pas/`.
impact: Future edits to `token_template_pas/sources/token_template.move` will NOT update the bytecode — silent drift between source and deployed bytecode.
recommendation: Add a script under `scripts/` that rebuilds `token_template_pas` and regenerates `BYTECODE_HEX`. Add a CI check that fails if the source has been modified without regenerating. At minimum, add a comment in getBytecode.ts citing the source file and a regeneration command.
